title: Berry on Linen
stainChemistry: Berries (blueberry, raspberry, blackberry, strawberry) carry anthocyanin pigments + tannin polyphenols + sugar. Anthocyanin is pH-sensitive (red in acid, blue-purple in alkaline) — alkaline cleaners DARKEN it. On linen (cellulose), tannin chemistry transfers from cotton (cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar). Linen-specific: shrinks under hot water, dyed linen bleeds, structured pieces warp under wet treatment. The standard tannin protocol works on simple flat linen; structured = pro.
whyThisWorks: Cold water + Dawn lifts fresh anthocyanin + sugar. 3% peroxide oxidizes residual tannin. Vinegar keeps anthocyanin red (light/acid side) instead of drifting blue-purple. Cool wash + air-dry flat preserves linen shape.
defaultAssumption: Simple flat linen napkin / shirt / plain skirt = home protocol. Structured / lined / trimmed / dry-clean-only / vintage = pro.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / trimmed / vintage = pro. Simple flat linen = proceed.
Flip inside out. Cold water through the BACK 1–2 min. Anthocyanin + sugar flush out fast.
Colored linen: hidden-seam test mandatory. Color transfer = pro. Skip for white linen.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cold water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn cold water on damp cloth, 5 min. Skip OxiClean — alkaline darkens anthocyanin permanently.
Hidden test on dyed linen first. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Don't mix with bleach / ammonia / alkaline.
Vinegar rinse. Keeps anthocyanin red (light) instead of drifting blue-purple.
Cool wash — NEVER hot (linen shrinks). Air-DRY FLAT. Inspect before any heat.

steps: 3. Hidden dye test on colored linen.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — linen shrinks under hot
warnings: NEVER hot water — linen shrinks aggressively
NEVER alkaline cleaners on tannin — darkens permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach on dyed linen
Hidden-area dye test mandatory on colored linen

homeSolutions.agent: Structured Linen Goes to Pro; Simple Linen Stays Home
homeSolutions.instruction: Linen has two cases. SIMPLE LINEN (napkin, basic shirt, plain skirt) = standard tannin home protocol. STRUCTURED LINEN (suits, lined dresses, tablecloths with stiffeners, dry-clean-only) = pro from minute one. Wet treatment risks shape change AND dyed linen bleeds easily.
Flip inside out. Cold tap water through the BACK for 1–2 minutes. Most effective fast move on berry stains.

homeSolutions.agent: Hidden Patch Test on Dyed Linen
homeSolutions.instruction: If colored, dab Dawn + cold water on a hidden seam, press 2 min. Color transfer = pro. Clean = proceed.
Dawn in cold water, soft toothbrush 5 min. NO OxiClean — alkaline turns berry anthocyanin DARKER permanently.
3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Hidden test on dyed linen first.
1 tbsp vinegar + 1 cup cool water, 5 min. Critical step on berries — vinegar keeps anthocyanin RED (light) instead of drifting blue-purple as it dries.
Cool wash. AIR-DRY FLAT. NEVER tumble-dry until fully clear — heat shrinks linen + sets berry tannin.

steps: 1. Structured / lined / trimmed = pro. Simple linen = home.
2. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach.
4. Hidden dye test on colored linen.
5. Dawn + cold water + soft toothbrush, 5 min.
7. White vinegar rinse — keeps anthocyanin RED.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if linen is structured, dye bleeds on hidden test, hot water / dryer was used previously.
warnings: NEVER chlorine bleach on dyed linen
Don't skip vinegar — keeps anthocyanin red (light), not blue-purple

neverDo: Never chlorine bleach on dyed linen
fiberSensitivities: Linen shrinks aggressively under hot water
Dyed linen bleeds — hidden test mandatory
Structured linen warps under wet treatment

materialWarnings: Vinegar keeps berry anthocyanin RED, not blue-purple

escalation.when: Structured / lined / trimmed linen, dyed linen failed hidden test, hot water / dryer used previously, vintage piece.
escalation.whatToTell: "Berry on [simple / structured / dyed / vintage] linen, [type], age [hours / days], home-treated with cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash. No hot water / no alkaline. [Residual / structured concerns]."

contextQuestions: Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Blueberry has the most anthocyanin — hardest. Strawberry has more sugar but less anthocyanin. Pre-applied OxiClean = anthocyanin darkened permanently into blue-purple.
